title: Microseris alpestris
taxonomy: Microseris alpestris is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower family. This classification is based on its characteristics and morphology, including its flower structure and seed production. Within the family Asteraceae, Microseris alpestris is further classified as a member of the genus Microseris, which contains several species of annual and perennial plants. The genus Microseris is part of the tribe Cichorieae, a group of plants known for their dandelion-like appearance and characteristics. The classification of Microseris alpestris reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the same family and genus.

The plant's roots and leaves contain various bioactive compounds that have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. intro: Microseris alpestris, also known as Mountain daisy or Alpine sweet cicely, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems, making it a valuable resource for maintaining overall health.
